body:
Bead: ddx-4b59b816 — Epic: cost-tiered execution + measurement

(a) What is done
- Read the bead description and acceptance criteria in full.
- Confirmed this is a standing tracking epic, not an implementation task.

(b) What is blocking
- Nothing is blocking. The bead is intentionally a backlog container.
- The acceptance criteria explicitly state the epic "stays open indefinitely
  as a backlog for cost-tiered execution and measurement work" and "Closes
  ONLY if DDx pivots away from this optimization goal entirely (unlikely)."
- Progress is measured indirectly via (a) child beads closed, (b) cost-per-
  closed-bead trends, (c) opus-review rejection-rate trends. None of these
  are satisfiable by a single execute-bead pass.
- The bead description further notes the listed child beads (per-bead cost
  aggregation, ladder success-rate analytics, prompt-context pre-filter,
  failure-mode taxonomy, review-rejection telemetry, dashboard) are
  deliberately NOT pre-filed "to avoid blocking the current Svelte+GraphQL
  migration." So filing them now would contradict the bead's own scoping.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
- Do not re-queue this epic for autonomous execution. It is a human-driven
  backlog marker. The orchestrator should leave it open and skip it.
- When the Svelte+GraphQL migration (project memory: locked 2026-04-14,
  4-stage epic) lands Stage 4, a human can file the listed child beads
  against this epic via `ddx bead create` with parent=ddx-4b59b816.
- Closing this bead from execute-loop would be incorrect; the AC forbids it
  absent an explicit pivot.

Recommendation: mark this bead as not-auto-executable (e.g., a label like
wave:deferred — already present — combined with a tracker rule that skips
epics from execute-loop) so future drains do not re-pick it.
